name: Red Chilli Chutney
description: Red Chilli Chutney is a Tamil Nadu style spicy condiment made with dry red chillies, coconut, roasted chana dal, ginger, garlic and tamarind. The tamarind adds a pleasant sourness that balances the heat from the chillies. Finished with a cracking tempering of mustard, cumin and curry leaves, this chutney is perfect with idli, dosa, uttapam, medu vada and bonda.

steps.instruction: Take grated coconut, roasted chana dal, dried red chillies, chopped ginger, garlic, tamarind and salt in a grinder jar.
Add ½ cup water and blend to a smooth and fine consistency.
steps.tip: Adjust the consistency by adding less or more water. Be mindful of salt when changing consistency.

steps.instruction: Transfer the chutney to a heat-proof bowl.
Heat oil in a small pan on low flame. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle.
Add cumin seeds and fry till they splutter.
Add asafoetida and curry leaves. Fry for a few seconds until curry leaves become crisp.
steps.tip: Skip asafoetida to make this chutney fully gluten-free.

steps.instruction: Switch off heat and pour the entire tempering over the chutney.
Mix well and serve with idli, dosa, uttapam, medu vada, bonda or pakoda.
steps.tip: This chutney is best consumed immediately after preparing.

tips: Use Kashmiri or Byadgi red chillies for a milder, brighter coloured chutney.
If using hot and pungent red chillies, reduce the quantity.
Skip asafoetida to make this chutney fully gluten-free.
Adjust the consistency by adding less or more water. Be mindful of salt when changing consistency.
You can add a few curry leaves while grinding for a unique taste.
Adding sambar onions/shallots while grinding gives an extra layer of flavour.
This chutney is best consumed immediately after preparing.
